classObjective: Determine whether a relation represented as a graph, table, mapping, set of ordered pairs, or equation is a function.
workedExamples: 3-2, Example 1 — Determine Whether a Relation Is a Function (Graph, Table, Mapping)
3-2, Example 2 — Determine Whether a Relation Is a Function from Real-World Data
warmUp: Use the vertical line test on a graph — what does passing or failing tell you?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: vertical line test for graphs; mapping diagram analysis; checking ordered pairs for repeated x-values
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice determining whether relations are functions using multiple methods
independentPractice: Practice problems determining function status using vertical line test, mapping diagrams, and ordered pair checking
assessment: Exit ticket: determine whether a given relation is a function using two different methods
capReflection: Why does the vertical line test work for determining if a graph represents a function?
